title: Stateline: 17+ ICE Open-Fire Incidents Since July 2025; DHS Accounts Contradicted
excerpt: A Stateline investigation found at least 17 open-fire incidents involving federal immigration agents since July 2025, documenting a pattern in which official DHS accounts of encounters were contradicted by bystander video and local law enforcement. The report also found DHS rules on shooting at moving vehicles diverge from best practices adopted by most major police departments.
content:
A January 2026 investigation by Stateline documented a surge in firearms discharges by federal immigration agents, compiling data from The Trace and a separate Wall Street Journal investigation.

  • Since July 2025, at least 17 open-fire incidents involved federal immigration agents — including fatal shootings, shootings with injuries, and cases in which shots were fired at civilians.
  • A Wall Street Journal investigation separately found 13 incidents since July in which immigration agents fired at or into civilian vehicles.
  • In multiple documented cases, DHS revised or contradicted its initial public narratives after bystander footage and local police accounts diverged from official statements — including the fatal shooting of Renee Good and a December shooting in Glen Burnie, Maryland.
  • DHS policy prohibits firing at the operator of a moving vehicle unless necessary to stop a serious threat, but does not explicitly instruct officers to move out of the path of moving vehicles — departing from what many major city police departments now treat as best practice.

The pattern of conflicting accounts raised questions about DHS's internal review process and the adequacy of use-of-force training for agents conducting at-large interior enforcement operations.
